title: FOUNDING — Brae Hollow young: the spring runs, the craft-row clatters
FOUNDING — Cistern Steps: Sera tends the upper spring and its stone catch-basin
FOUNDING — Sera finds a creeping rot working into the rock at the cistern's lower vent
FOUNDING — Sera packs and seals the cistern's lower vent against the rot
FOUNDING — Sera cuts her keeper's mark beside the sealed vent so a later hand will know it was tended
FOUNDING — the cistern runs sweet again; Sera's work at the Steps is done
FOUNDING — Loom Quarter: the dyers, weavers, and Halden the boundary-mason at the craft-row
FOUNDING — a craft-row dispute over which crossing the dye-carts may use
FOUNDING — Halden reads the river and judges which ford will stay firm when the channel shifts
FOUNDING — Halden cuts a boundary-stone to mark the firm ford
FOUNDING — Halden sets the boundary-stone at the head of the firm ford
FOUNDING — the dispute settled; Halden never imagines a drought, only the carts
FOUNDING — Wend Fields: the lower farmland green, the river crossing busy
FOUNDING — the bearer arrives in the young hollow carrying the carved bone wend-pipe
FOUNDING — at the Cistern Steps the bearer watches Sera at the sealed vent
FOUNDING — at the Loom Quarter the bearer watches Halden set the boundary-stone
FOUNDING — the bearer learns the wend-pipe moves them between the two ages on one spot
FOUNDING — the bearer walks down to the Wend Fields and the river crossing
FOUNDING — at the crossing a dry gorge cuts the way to the far field across the channel
FOUNDING — a field-elder gives the bearer a slow gorge-sapling and tells what it could become
FOUNDING — the bearer plays the wend-pipe and stands a moment in the withered hollow to come
FOUNDING — the bearer crosses back and understands the early age is the late age's cause
FOUNDING — the bearer returns to the Wend crossing with the gorge-sapling in hand
FOUNDING — the bearer weighs whether the far field is worth the planting
FOUNDING — the bearer kneels at the lip of the dry gorge at dusk
FOUNDING — THE CHOICE at the Wend crossing: plant the gorge-sapling, or do not
PLANTED — Withering Age: three generations on, the long drought lies on Brae Hollow
PLANTED — Cistern Steps: Marn the well-tender keeps the last good water in the valley
PLANTED — Marn does not know why the cistern still runs clean when every other spring failed
PLANTED — the bearer crosses to the Steps and recognises the same vent Sera packed
PLANTED — the cistern, sealed three generations gone, still runs clean: the last good water
PLANTED — Loom Quarter gone quiet: Pell the weaver works the last loom on the dead craft-row
PLANTED — Pell knows nothing of the old dispute or who cut any boundary-stone
PLANTED — Wend Fields: Ode the field-warden keeps the crossing for the Withering folk
PLANTED — Ode finds Halden's boundary-stone half-buried at the shifted channel and reads it
PLANTED — the Withering folk cross to the last green field on the firm ford the stone marks
PLANTED — the gorge-sapling has grown to a living bridge-tree spanning the dry gorge
PLANTED — Tamsin the far-field settler reaches and works the far field across the bridge-tree
PLANTED — the bearer crosses to the Founding Age and witnesses why the cistern and ford hold
PLANTED ending — Brae Hollow withers but the far field carries it: water, ford, and bridge hold
BARREN — Withering Age: three generations on, the long drought lies on Brae Hollow
BARREN — Cistern Steps: Marn the well-tender keeps the last good water in the valley
BARREN — Marn does not know why the cistern still runs clean when every other spring failed
BARREN — the bearer crosses to the Steps and recognises the same vent Sera packed
BARREN — the cistern, sealed three generations gone, still runs clean: the last good water
BARREN — Loom Quarter gone quiet: Pell the weaver works the last loom on the dead craft-row
BARREN — Pell knows nothing of the old dispute or who cut any boundary-stone
BARREN — Wend Fields: Ode the field-warden keeps the crossing for the Withering folk
BARREN — Ode finds Halden's boundary-stone half-buried at the shifted channel and reads it
BARREN — the Withering folk cross to the near green field on the firm ford the stone marks
BARREN — at the gorge nothing grew: the crossing-place to the far field is bare rock
BARREN — the dry gorge is impassable and the far field across it is lost to the hollow
BARREN — the bearer crosses to the Founding Age and witnesses why the cistern and ford hold
BARREN ending — Brae Hollow withers on the near field alone; the far field stays lost
